title: University's Employability Support and Global Rankings
text: Career advancement We are extremely proud of our employability support. By preparing students to be career-ready, as well as earn their globally ranked university degree, we help open up a world of employment and entrepreneurship once they graduate. World class careers and opportunity We have a strong reputation for producing top graduate talent as students start their employment journey with leading firms. Many will start their own businesses, with 42% expressing an interest in entrepreneurship. Our Successful Futures programme encourages students to reflect on their skills throughout their studies and through real-world experience. And partnerships with respected and well-known employers guarantee employability opportunities for students. Global 'Top 110' best university for employability Source: QS Graduate Employability Rankings 2022 UK's #15 university for graduate prospects Source: Times and Sunday Times Good University Guide 2025 UK's #10 university targeted by top employers Source: The Graduate Market in 2023 by High Fliers Research
summary: University of Southampton Delhi provides extensive employability support, preparing students for careers and entrepreneurship. It is recognized among top universities globally and in the UK for graduate prospects and employer targets.
entities: Times and Sunday Times Good University Guide 2025
The Graduate Market in 2023 by High Fliers Research

title: Successful Futures Programme and Skills Model Overview
text: Successful Futures Research shows that the more students plan their careers during their time at university, the more likely it is they will get a graduate-level job or move into further study. As a result, each programme at the university has employability awareness and development embedded through an approach that we call Successful Futures. The Successful Futures approach delivers a uniquely Southampton skills model through both an online self-assessment and a personalised employability and enterprise learner journey. Through it, among other, we focus on equal access to careers education and on preparing students for lifelong learning by empowering them to identify, reflect on and develop their employability and enterprise skills. Our skills model The University of Southampton skills model comprises 14 skills which have been identified by research with employers, academic colleagues, students and graduates as key skills graduate recruiters want. All our degree programmes have been mapped against the skills model.Each student undertakes a skills self-assessment online at start of their journey with the university and on an annual basis thereafter. This digital resource was developed using global recruitment data and is designed to encourage students to explore career ideas, consider their career choices, reflect on skills gained and signpost opportunities for skills development. We believe that if students have awareness of what skills employers want for their career choice, which skills they are developing in their course and where they can find opportunities to develop skills further, they will be better prepared for a graduate recruitment market that places increasing emphasis on skills rather than simply on qualifications.
summary: The Successful Futures programme integrates employability development into every degree, utilizing a unique Southampton skills model and online self-assessment to prepare students for the graduate job market.

title: Structured Student Career Support and Outcomes
text: A structured approach for better outcomes We support students to:-use the Skills Self-Assessment to identify and reflect on employability skills they are developing throughout their programme of study.identify, and plan how to acquire additional skills and experience they may want or need to help them meet their career aspirations.undertake the work experience, international semester option and enterprise support to prepare for their future.successfully articulate their skills to graduate recruiters or in pitching for funding as a start-up.use intensive job coaching to be fully prepared for guaranteed interviews with top graduate recruiters.So that when engaging with employers students will:-have already worked on industry problems via live briefs, increasing their commercial awareness.have had real world experience from internships, gaining understanding of what is needed in the workplace.have gained international experience, gaining greater awareness of other cultures and marketsbe fully prepared for recruitment interviews, helping employers identify the best fit for their needs.be better able to clearly articulate their skills to graduate recruiters or in pitching for funding as a start-up.
summary: The university provides structured career support, including skills self-assessment, work experience, international options, job coaching, and interview preparation, to ensure students are ready for the graduate market.

title: E-Mentoring Southampton for Career Guidance
text: Plan your career with E-Mentoring Southampton E-Mentoring Southampton connects you with more than 2,500 industry mentors around the world for expert career advice and support in finding your first graduate job. All E-Mentors are Southampton alumni and supporters, who are waiting to hear from you and help you with your career questions.Ask Southampton graduates questions about their career and receive real-world answers from the experts.Connect with industry mentors working for companies and sectors you’re interested in.Build a professional network with a global community of University of Southampton alumni.Plan your career with the support and expertise of our mentor community.
summary: E-Mentoring Southampton connects students with over 2,500 global alumni mentors, offering expert career advice, industry connections, and professional networking opportunities to aid in job searching.

title: Global Graduate Success and Top Employers (Part 1)
text: A tradition of success in India and worldwide University of Southampton has a long and proud tradition of seeing graduates start successful careers in India and worldwide locations, with globally recognised companies in a variety of market sectors. We have been proud to empower our graduates to join these top names over recent years:-Aerospace, transport and automotiveAirbus - The world's leading helicopter and airliner manufacturer.Alstom - French multinational rolling stock manufacturer which operates worldwide in rail transport markets.Rolls-Royce - Multinational aerospace and defense company that designs, manufactures, and distributes power systems for aviation and other industries.Toyota - Japanese multinational automotive manufacturer.Financial services and bankingAmerican Express - American bank holding company and multinational financial services corporation that specializes in payment cards.First Abu Dhabi Bank - Largest bank in the United Arab Emirates.JP Morgan - Global financial services firm, offering a wide range of services including investment banking, asset management, and private banking.Metlife - One of the world's leading financial services companies, providing insurance, annuities, employee benefits and asset management.
summary: University of Southampton graduates achieve successful careers globally, joining leading companies across various sectors like aerospace and finance, including Airbus, Rolls-Royce, JP Morgan, and American Express.

title: Extensive Graduate Employer List by Sector (Part 2)
text: EducationGandhi Institute of Technology and Management (GITAM) - Private university in Visakhapatnam, Hyderabad and Bengalur.Ghent University - Public research university in the East Flanders province of Belgium.Royal Institute of Technology (KTH) - One of Europe's leading technical and engineering universities.University of Hawaii - Major teaching and research university in the US.University of Heidelberg - Internationally networked German university dedicated to research-oriented teaching.EnergySchlumberger (SLB) - Global technology company that provides technology and services to the oil and gas industry, as well as exploring new energy systems.Shell - A global group of energy and petrochemical companies.Public servicesNational Health Service (NHS) - Publicly funded healthcare system in the UK.Professional services and consultingCapgemini - Gobal business and technology transformation partner.Deloitte - A 'Big Four' multinational professional services network based in the UK.KPMG - A 'Big Four' global professional services network that offers audit, tax, and advisory services.PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) - Multinational professional services network and one of the "Big Four" accounting firms globally.SportsMclaren Racing - Motor racing team based in the UK.Red Bull Racing - Formula One racing team based in the UK.Technology and manufacturingABB - Swedish-Swiss multinational electrical engineering corporation known for its work in electrification and automation, including robotics.ARM Holdings - Semiconductor and software design company headquartered in the UK.Citygroup - One of Bangladesh's leading conglomerates and trusted consumer goods manufacturers.Dyson - Innovative design and manufactures of household appliances.Sparkbox - Partners with complex organizations to create user-driven digital experiences.Verizon - World's second-largest telecomms company, and largest wireless carrier in the US.
summary: University of Southampton graduates secure positions across diverse sectors including education with institutions like GITAM and Ghent University, energy with Shell, public services with NHS, and technology with companies like ABB and Verizon.
entities: Gandhi Institute of Technology and Management (GITAM)

title: Employment Partnerships and Global Networks
text: Employment partnerships Our curricula incorporate global perspectives through case studies, projects, and literature. Internships, placements and career guidance are extensive, recognising their importance to students and families. We seek the same qualities in all our campuses regardless of location - academic excellence, intellectual curiosity, strong ethical values, and a willingness to tackle global challenges. We value students who have an international outlook and an open mind, since we are shaping future global leaders. Global and local networks Airbus - Aerospace AstraZeneca - Pharma and biotech Boeing - Aerospace BAE - Aerospace and security Comviva - Telecomms Deloitte - Audit and consulting gsk - Pharma and biotech IBM - Tech and consulting Investec - Financial services Johnson & Johnson - Pharma and biotech L.E.K. - Consulting and strategy Lloyd's Register - Professional and maritime services Rolls-Royce - Aerospace and defence Shell - Energy and petrochemicals Siemens - Tech conglomerate
summary: The university integrates global perspectives, internships, and career guidance into its curricula, fostering future global leaders through partnerships with leading companies like Airbus, Deloitte, and Shell.
